Title: No Package, No Refund, No Help
Text: I absolutely love your brand, and after finally being able to afford to buy something from you, I've had a **HORRIBLE** experience. Do you have my clothes? Or my money? Because I have neither. You entered my address incorrectly (I've already verified that the address I provided on your website was 100% correct). When the package arrived in Spain, it couldn't be delivered because of the incorrect address. I contacted your customer support, and you told me you had reached out to the courier and that the issue had been resolved. Imagine my surprise when the courier informed me that **nobody** had provided them with a corrected address, and that the package had been returned to the sender. I've been trying to contact you, but of course I have to wait 24–48 hours for a reply and I'm told not to send any more emails in the meantime. You haven't offered me any solution whatsoever, and you don't even reply to me on social media. So here I am, looking like a complete fool, with no money and no clothes—which were supposed to be a birthday present. Thanks for nothing.

I have been waiting since July 1st for a resolution, was told it would take almost a week to get an answer to a simple question of replacing the item I had not received because the delivery driver put it in a ‘safe place’ that doesn’t exist on my property or with a neighbour. I was asked if I wanted a replacement today as the partner has agreed to it and Now I’ve replied to an agents question on the app, the ai bot has started the whole process again? I used to use boohoo all the time but I have plenty choice words I could use to describe the service I have received since the item was dispatched on June 25th?! I want a refund and to speak to somebody on a live chat, not waiting days inbetween messages. Absolutely atrocious service and I’ll be taking my money elsewhere in future.

Title: DO NOT BUY FROM THIS COMPANY!
Text: I placed an order a few months ago but was disappointed with the poor quality of the items, so I contacted customer services to arrange a return. I was required to pay an additional £6 to return the goods. At no point was I advised that I needed to obtain or retain a tracking number. After several weeks without any acknowledgement that my return had been received or my refund processed, I contacted them to chase an update. I was then told that no refund would be issued unless I could provide a tracking number—which I no longer had, as I had never been instructed to keep one. Despite months of back-and-forth communication, I have still not received a refund, nor have the returned items been sent back to me. Extremely poor customer service and a complete lack of accountability. I am now out of pocket for both the order and the return postage. Avoid.

Title: Last Boohoo Order - Returns Policy
Text: I won’t be ordering from Boohoo nor any concession within its wider Debenhams parent group again, on principle. Having paid £3.99 for delivery, I was sent the incorrect item. However, to return this item cost me a further £1.99 which will eventually be deducted from the refund amount I will receive. The customer should not have to pay an additional (item return) charge for Boohoo’s incompetence in its basic retail activity of selecting and sending the correct product that was ordered. While £1.99 appears miniscule, it nonetheless represents a 50% increase in delivery charges. If this occurs on a regular basis across the entire Debenhams retail group, however, it is a great money making ruse where the customer ends up with nothing but the company inflates income made from delivery charges in reward for its own potentially deliberate error.

Title: Used Items Resold as New
Text: I had my eye on a Ralph Lauren jumper for a while and couldn't find it anywhere else. It finally came back into stock during a sale, so I ordered it straight away. When it arrived, it was covered in pet hair, smelled strongly of aftershave, had no tags attached, had visible marks, and several pulled threads. It clearly did not appear to be in the condition I would expect for a new item. I spent over an hour speaking with customer services and explained that I would be happy to keep the jumper if a partial refund could be offered to reflect its condition, especially as it is now sold out and cannot be replaced. However, I was told the only option was to return it for inspection, and a refund would only be considered if it was deemed to have a manufacturing fault. The most frustrating part is that if I return it and it is simply put back into stock, another customer could end up receiving the same item. Very disappointing experience and not what I would expect when purchasing a premium brand item. -------------------- **UPDATE:** I returned the item, and when it came back into stock for £80 more, I bought it again. To my disbelief, I have been sent the exact same faulty item. ASOS had clearly brushed the item in an attempt to remove the original mark, but the stitching across the back was still missing. I was specifically told to return it as faulty, and I even included a note clearly highlighting the defect to make sure it was not sent to another customer—especially as someone could easily wear it before noticing the missing stitching and then find themselves unable to return it. After paying £80 more, waiting over a week for the order to be dispatched, and expecting a replacement, I have now received the exact same defective item back. Absolutely appalling.

Title: ASOS used to be my go-to online…
Text: ASOS used to be my go-to online retailer for trendy clothing and accessories, but it has gone downhill. Delivery is no longer truly “express —even Premier members often wait two or more days for their orders. The quality of the clothing has also noticeably declined. My biggest issue is ASOS’s Fair Trade Policy. Because ASOS has no physical stores, customers have to order clothing to try it on. Yet ASOS monitors how much you return and can restrict or even close your account based on your return rate, without giving you a meaningful opportunity to explain yourself. That’s simply not fair when many returns are due to sizing or fit—something you can’t know until you’ve received and tried on the items. A fair policy should recognize the reality of online shopping, not penalize loyal customers for ordering multiple sizes or styles to find the right fit. Between the slower shipping, declining quality, and this restrictive Fair Trade Policy, I’ve found other online retailers now offer a much better customer experience.

Title: Gone to the dogs
Text: I’ve been using asos for 15 plus years and it has gone down hill majorly and I’m not surprised people are shopping elsewhere. But I ordered something in an emergency for a wake as someone had passed suddenly and there was a colour theme. It wasn’t delivered on time as they use crappy Royal Mail who everyone knows are terrible. You pay for premium so your NDD is free ..  so if you don’t get your NDD you get nothing in compensation… but if you pay for NDD they state they will refund you. So basically you get treated worse as a premium customer with zero care or compensation. 48 hours after ordering a NDD… and 3 hours to go before the event and I have no items or clothing to wear suitable for the occasion. If you return items because they weren’t as pictured on the website ( like the dress I bought last week) or because it didn’t arrive on time they will penalise you for returning even if it’s their fault your returning it! Customer care do not care and gone are the days when you could email them and receive a genuine response and help. They are terrible and the main reason I used them is as for the NDD which by my experience and the many reviews on here is a non existent service anymore. Even shein have better customer service than this! No point at all being a premier customer when you get zero help whatsoever and NDD does not happen . All they tell you to do is wait for the item and if you don’t want it to refuse the delivery ( but yet they use delivery services who just chuck your parcel outside so you can’t even do this ) Awful!

Title: ASOS Return Process Is Unreliable and Unprofessional
Text: I am extremely disappointed with ASOS and their return handling process. This is now the second time that a return has been rejected on the grounds that the item was allegedly “used,” “washed,” and “smelling of detergent.” This is simply not true. It makes absolutely no sense to wash an item that I intend to return because it does not fit. There is clearly a serious issue within ASOS’s warehouse or return inspection process. Having the same problem occur twice is not a coincidence—it points to a systemic failure. As a result, I am now left without a refund for items that were returned in proper condition. Given the amount involved, I will be pursuing legal action through my legal protection insurance to recover my money. This situation is unacceptable. ASOS’s handling of customer returns is unprofessional and raises serious concerns about how they treat their customers.

They also advised that they never give QR codes for partner brands even though I had been given them in the past.  I asked to make a complaint and they told me that they were going to end the chat so I could not continue to talk with them. They are absolutely unbelievable! Thieves! I will work on deleting my account now and make sure to never buy frkm this awful branc@

Title: Lied about offering me free express shipping and charged me for it
Text: Due to them damaging my package, they advised me if I paid for express shipping of €15.00 euro for the next package they would refund me it. Fast forward and they said they wouldn’t be able to do that even though I had all the proof they said to re order and would….

Title: Terrible experience from start to…it
Text: Terrible experience from start to finish. Placed order middle of May, received no order confirmation, PayPal took the payment immediately. Received no notification. Or order being sent. Waited few days contacted ASOS on chat and told order wasn’t received there end and must of been a computer glitch, I advised payment taken and was told would need to raise with PayPal and place order again. I placed order again and raised dispute with PayPal, Piecemeal responses received from ASOS through the PayPal dispute and then my original order suddenly arrives on the doorstep closely followed by my second order. Tried to return my one order as don’t need 2 and most painful experience ever trying to get this sorted as ASOS had no record of my order so didn’t show an order number in my account, then sent me QR codes that were to big to print off or screenshot. Finally got it right and retuned order, no refund received, waited the 2 weeks quoted for returns to be made contacted ASOS again on chat and after another extensive painful exercise to locate my return I was advised my refund had failed! No explanation as to what this meant or timeline given as to when my monies will be received I was simply told it would be looked into and escalated and I would hear something by email. Absolutely disgusting, still no refund or response from ASOS. Trading standards now being contacted and will be pursuing for compensation for breach of ASOS company terms and any interest lost from delayed payment. I would advise anyone thinking or ordering with this company to avoid at all costs they haven’t got a clue what they are doing and the customer service support is non existent

Title: Been a loyal asos user for a long time
Text: Been a loyal asos user for a long time. Customer service was always great. Used to be "wrong item sent? Sorry about that heres a gift card for the inconvenience", now its "wrong item sent? Hahaha you have to pay for the return because your return "score" is too low because we keep sending things incorrectly! Hahaha". Standard delivery 3-5 days you say? Oh we'll send it whenever we feel like, and you cant cancel it so just send it back at your own expense and we'll take the money for delivery and also lower your "score" even more despite not fulfilling our side of the sale agreement. Greedy and sloppy.

Title: Six stars!
Text: As always, an excellent experience with Ranapui. Exceptional products and great customer service. Highly recommended.

Title: They really need to allow us zero star…
Text: They really need to allow us zero star option on here! What can I say about ASOS??? Avoid it? Don't waste your money? They lie? They cheat? They mislead customers? Just be careful using ASOS. If you can go with another provider then do so.

Title: Avoid! Item didn’t arrive & poor customer service
Text: I ordered a bag for my daughter’s prom well in advance, but it never arrived. Boohoo’s online‑only support was slow and mostly automated, and despite asking for a replacement, they only offered a refund. After weeks of chasing, the refund finally came — but no bag, no urgency, and no accountability. I won’t order from them again .

Title: missing order and refusing to refund!!!!!
Text: I ordered 3 dresses from them and they never arrived. I have contacted them and its only a stupid ai or chat function and you can't speak to a real person.They keep saying someone will get back to me  but dont. I now have to contact my bank as ive ordered may even be fraudulent.  they never used to be this bad. Don't use. they are SO Awful!!!!!!

Title: failed deliveries
Text: Placed an order, delivery failed twice despite me being at the delivery address. Parcel returned to ASOS with no option to get it back to me. I have to wait for the return to be processed and then re-order. No customer service available other than Ai chat. Won't be using Asos again

Title: Cannot cancel order
Text: Order placed on Sunday, 3-5 working days delivery, means Wednesday to Friday, right? Wrong, it’s now Thursday  and there’s order hasn’t updated at all. Contacted ASOS and they said they cannot cancel the order as it moves quickly through an automated robotic picking system that they cannot intervene with. But it hasn’t moved quickly if it’s still not dispatched! They then kept saying it’s in transit so cannot cancel, but it’s not in transit. Story kept changing. All I wanted was to cancel the order. Simple request.

Title: The quality of ASOS clothing has…
Text: The quality of ASOS clothing has declined significantly over the past few years, while prices have continued to increase. Many items feel cheaply made, and the sizing is often inconsistent, which results in more returns. Another issue is that many products don’t have customer reviews. Reviews are essential when shopping online because they help customers decide on the quality and fit before purchasing. Without them, it’s much harder to make the right choice. ASOS has now started charging me for returns because my return rate is considered too high. However, when the quality is poor, sizing is inconsistent, and there aren’t enough reviews to help customers make informed decisions, it’s unfair to penalise customers for returning unsuitable items. I used to buy and keep far more items in the past, but the decline in quality has made me shop there much less.

Title: Openly breaking UK law
Text: So my premier was set to auto renew and they took payment. I hopped on chat to cancel. Their website states within one month and not used it you can cancel. The chat advisor said the same thing. The chat adviser then proceed to refuse to refund and instead tried to trick me into cancelling it from auto renewing in a years time. I explained I wanted a refund and for the impending membership not to renew. The chat support said they couldn't do that. I stated it was my right under UK law to cancel.  They said they would not refund Will be doing a charge back and reporting then to trading standards.

Title: What jokers! 😡
Text: I ordered a belt for a birthday gift, paid £4.50 for postage nearly a week later still not here. Had no confirmation email until yesterday!! Now they are saying it’s running late. I now find out I have to wait another week!!!! NO ONE CARES!!! Looking at all these bad reviews I’m shocked that they are actually still running 🤷🏻‍♀️ I will never use them again & would advise everyone else not to bother either! What a joke!!!!

Title: What has happened to ASOS
Text: They have gone down hill lately, I always have credit balance due to gift cards and have never had issues with delivery till now. You cannot get next day so why pay the subscription. Such a shame as used to be so good.

Title: disgusting customer service
Text: disgusting customer service, delayed my order, refused to let me cancel it as order delayed after their cancellation window, refused to exempt me from having to pay the return delivery fee, their whole return rate % is appalling anyway as their size guides are never accurate. ASOS do not care about their customers what so ever. will never order from them again.

Title: A COMPANY THAT DOESN'T CARE
Text: Almost 200,000 reviews, all of them awful, (and not one review replied to as you would expect with a decent  professional company), says it all. The demise of ASOS cannot be far away, what a shame. ASOS dropped almost their entire 'Tall' range 2-3 years ago, and now do not even offer a tall size t shirt, long or short sleeve. I used to spend over £1000 a year with ASOS, now however I cannot order a single thing because ASOS no longer sell tall staples.

Title: Horrible experience and not sure if…
Text: Horrible experience and not sure if incompetence of customer service or Asos has bad technical support. I lost my access to social media, and this is how I used to log in in to my Asos account where I already payed for premier delivery and Asos customer service said that there is nothing they can do to recover my account. Then and there I decided to delete their app and never gonna order from them again. How is it possible a company so big it does not have offer support with this issue? It is absolutely unacceptable.

Title: Not impressed at all
Text: Not impressed at all! I pay for next day delivery on an order only for it to go missing, and I will not have the shipping company blamed for it because I use them on a daily basis and there are amazing. When I finally got through to a bot I did manage to get it sorted only for the item to arrive looking quite frankly used screwed up in a ball and not worthy of a jumble sale.

Title: I ordered 2 dresses after moving house
Text: I ordered 2 dresses after moving house. I saw on the order the address was my old one so I amended. However when the order went through it showed my old address. I contacted customer service immediately to amend the order and they said I couldn’t, despite there being no way it had been packed. They said I had to contact the new tenants to pick it up and if they chose to keep the items there is nothing they can do. It’s absolutely ridiculous that there isn’t any way or even amending the address once bought and I’ve probably lost all of that money now. They escalated it to someone who just said the exact same thing. Very frustrating. Definitely won’t shop here again.

Title: They charge a refund fee for a faulty…
Text: They charge a refund fee for a faulty item LOL, customer has to pay a fee, for a refunded faulty item, what a JOKE of a company, customer care is also litteral garbage, was told by a employee to search the information for myself, so what’s the point of having customer care?

Title: Boohoo has massively gone down hill
Text: Boohoo has massively gone down hill! I purchased an item on their website - this was then delivered by a concessions partner of theirs. Long story short , I didn’t like the item and needed to return it (unworn) was told to contact the concessions partner directly (SVNX) who refused my return and stopped replying to my emails. Why? Because it supposedly says on their website that you cant make returns to fascinators. Why would I have looked on their website when it was purchased via Boohoo? lol Not to mention everytime I really to Boohoo to their customer service chat a new person responds and asks the same questions over and over which is MADDENING!! I have now gotten this money back via my bank but I would NEVER purchase from boohoo again and u shouldn’t too unless u want stress and to be robbed

Title: So nice to have sizes that fit well
Text: So nice to have sizes that fit well, not like other companies where size M is now more like size S. Lovely quality and ethos.

Title: Easy help very simple and they respond…
Text: Easy help very simple and they respond fast and it helps alot thank you kai for your very good job keep it going!

Title: I wouldn't even give them 1 star
Text: I wouldn't even give them 1 star! I placed an order on 28 June and paid for expedited delivery. As of today (1 July) I receive an email telling me they are busier than usual so my parcel won't arrive until 6th July - the day I go on holiday. Their customer service reps refuse to help and refuse to cancel the order because "I have not met their cancellation deadlines" - how can I meet a deadline that they themselves have broken! So the parcel will arrive and no doubt be thrown on my doorstep. I then have to wait until after my holiday to pay to return the parcel and wait for a refund!! I tried to make a complaint but apparently they don't acknowledge complaints, they only take it as "feedback". Fine, my complaint will be sent to whatever senior persons I can find online. I am sick of this company, they have all their demands re. Return % rates and charges, but cannot even meet delivery times for people who placed orders in good time and faith. Worst thing is - they will allow me to place the order again and select "next day delivery"!!! So I'll have to pay another delivery fee, wait on a return and pay the return fee. Asos are a horrible, disgusting company who wont have any more orders from me. The sooner people stop using them and play them at their own game, the better.

Title: Great customer service from Kai
Text: Great customer service from Kai. Question answered quickly. Hopefully no problems with my order now.

Title: Top Notch!
Text: The customer service I received was top notch. Very quick responses to every email, very helpful, supportive and sympathetic and a fab resolution. Couldn’t have been better. Thanks again.

Title: The worst company
Text: The worst company, returns are a scam the warehouse was closed and they did not accept the return delivery DO NOT ORDER FROM HERE

Title: Kai was very helpful and quick to…
Text: Kai was very helpful and quick to respond to my queries

Title: I bought a long gilet from ASOS in…
Text: I bought a long gilet from ASOS in January and  after a few months the zip stopped working. So I contacted them and said I know I was out of the refund policy however it should last a lot longer than a few months So they told me to return it which I did . They then said as you are out of the return policy and it smells of perfume and has dirt on they can’t do anything and returned it to me. It did smell of perfume but was not dirty and they knew it had been worn.I was very disappointed with the customer service and thought a large brand like that would be more accommodating. It has put me off shopping there again .

Title: Never again
Text: The service seem nice, however the overall experience is awful. Paid extra for next day delivery, but they sent the wrong item. But when returned there was a fee deducted for the return, and the delivery cost was over looked. Nevertheless, still havent received the refund of what ever is left despite being assured the refund is on its way. Never again with boohoo. Such a shame.

Title: Contacted Boohoo on there WhatsApp…
Text: Contacted Boohoo on there WhatsApp group I got a response straight away with a very helpful response and the question answered quickly, great customer service from Kai

Title: Appalling customer services/ refund issues
Text: I have been an ASOS customer for years. It’s my go to website for buying clothing.  I recently purchased four prom dresses for my daughter knowing that I would be sending three back! Two were sent back with no issues, the third which was purchased through their marketplace didn’t have a returns slip so emailed ASOS and they said would have to contact the company to which o said I have no details, ASOS then sent me the form to send back to them and they would send on-this process took a good few days as nothing seems to get resolved easily! Fast forward four weeks and finally they have stated that they have refunded my account but they haven’t! They used an old account which I haven’t used in years and are refusing to refund into the bank account (active) on my ASOS! I’m so frustrated with all the ai chats (which I have screenshotted) I just want my money back! I will no longer being using ASOS after this as the customer service has been absolutely diabolical!

Title: Awful!
Text: Awful! Everytime I order lately, there is an issue with the return. They say you have returned less items than you have, try to give you less money, and also try to give you credit when you have opted for original payment method! There on the scam! No contact number, no live chat. When you message, they don’t even read the messages properly. I’m going to be closing my account. Too much hassle, shouldn’t be this hard when your return.

Title: I requested a return and followed TOS's…
Text: I requested a return and followed TOS's instructions exactly. Their customer service told me to return the parcel to one of their partner stores, but they never informed me that I should obtain a tracking number or any proof of return. The store also did not provide me with any receipt. The parcel was lost after I handed it over to their authorized return partner. Now TOS refuses to refund or compensate me, even though I followed their return process exactly as instructed. I believe customers should not be held responsible for failures in a company's own return system. This has been a very disappointing experience, and I hope TOS improves its return process and customer support.

Title: My first order was returned to ASOS…
Text: My first order was returned to ASOS because of an issue involving their shipping partner, APG eCommerce, and I never received my package. I gave ASOS another chance and placed a second order, but the exact same thing happened again. My order was returned instead of being delivered. What makes this even more frustrating is that I still have not received my refund. I’ve tried contacting support through the AI chatbot multiple times, but I have not received any useful information or a clear update on my case. I also couldn’t find a way to contact them directly by email, which made resolving the issue feel impossible. After two failed orders, no successful delivery, and ongoing difficulty getting support and a refund, this has been an extremely disappointing experience. Based on what happened, I will not be ordering from ASOS again.

Title: I ordered a couple of items in a size…
Text: I ordered a couple of items in a size 14. The boxy t shirt was ok but the boat neck top was far too big. I returned this asking for a size 12 replacement. When the replacement arrived they had replaced the t shirt. I contacted customer service and they got back to me saying correct replacement would be sent and to keep the wrongly supplied t shirt. Customer service was excellent in correcting this over and above. Thank you.

Title: Love this clothing line
Text: The products are of amazing quality. Love this store. Shipping to Canada took a while, but tracking was available to see it on its way.

Title: Worst Refund Process ever for boohoo mistake
Text: Boohoo Australia sent me my wedding shoes in UK sizing so they were huge, I tried to explain the mistake and eventually they sent me a label to return, it’s been a month with no refund and when emailing them they asked for proof of return, for a label they sent me. Still no refund, very frustrating, I won’t ever purchase from them again.

Title: Refund not issued
Text: I bought a few dresses to look at for a wedding, all from ASOS.  When they arrived they were from a variety of 3rd parties.  So when I went to return them I had to send back multiple parcels and check each seller was refunding me.  To refund one of them (NA-KD) I contacted ASOS to ask what to do as this retailer's information was not on their website.  They told me to use the return label provided and said I needed to do nothing else. When I then did not get the refund I contacted ASOS who said without proof of postage they wouldn't track this item for me.  I pointed out that the return label was provided by the seller so they would have that information and be able to track it but they point blank refused.  I explained the previous ASOS chat colleague had not said I needed to get the proof of postage, but again they just said there was nothing they could do.  If they can't even track what their 3rd party are doing or hole them accountable then this is not a provider I will ever use again.

Title: I am absolutely disgusted by this…
Text: I am absolutely disgusted by this company, I’ve been a loyal customer for many years. I will never shop with ASOS again as long as I live. The two items in question were not worn. In fact, they were not even tried on. They were removed from the packaging, inspected, and immediately repackaged because the quality was so poor that I knew straight away they were being returned. To suggest that they have been “used” is completely false. They were returned in the exact same condition they were sent to me, and they were sent back within 24 hours of receiving them. I refuse to accept that I should have to pay for items that were returned immediately and have not been worn. Your allegation that they are in a “used and unsuitable condition” is simply untrue, and I expect you to provide clear photographic evidence showing exactly what you believe constitutes this so-called damage or use. A generic rejection is not acceptable. This has been an incredibly disappointing experience. Not only was the quality of the items far below what was advertised, but now I am being accused of returning worn clothing when that is categorically untrue. I expect this decision to be reviewed immediately and my refund processed without any further delay. If you continue to refuse my refund without providing evidence, I will have no hesitation in escalating this matter further through my payment provider and the relevant consumer protection channels. Frankly, your handling of this matter is unacceptable. I’m fuming. Rejection reason: Item returned in a used and unsuitable condition

Title: My package was being delivered to the…
Text: My package was being delivered to the wrong address (which I had forgotten to change). I tried contacting customer service, and was redirected to their website or live chat. Their website is as not helpful as it did not have my problem on their questions. When I went to live chat (which is just a AI bot by the way) it was not helpful at all. In the past when this has happened to me to other companies like Space NK, the staff were soo helpful and I was able to get the problem sorted out straight away. I think it’s ridiculous a big company like ASOS does not have a solution to this or even real people that can help me out with my problem or even other niche problems.

Title: Awful company to deal with
Text: Bought 4 items. Returned 3. They charged me £1.99 to return each item plus the £4.99 which I knew id have to pay. Took over a month to get a refund on 1 item because it came from a third party. You cannot contact them only by WhatsApp or messenger. You might be lucky to get a real person and then they don’t understand you. I did email them through a complaints email. I’ve now asked them to delete my account (guess what I’m still waiting for a reply) I will never use this company again, absolutely appalling to deal with.

Title: I ordered some shoes a couple of months…
Text: I ordered some shoes a couple of months ago and the wrong size came I sent them back and didn’t get a refund Still waiting for a refund after the 2nd and 3rd attempts will not be using boohoo again

Title: Horrendous customer service
Text: I used to love boohoo, but they have gone so downhill. The quality is often poor but the main problem is their appalling customer service. You can't speak to anyone, you can't contact a manager on chat. You are faced with bots and then humans who just don't listen to what you are saying. After I finally manage to get rid of the voucher I own, I won't be shopping with them again. I bought several items with a £30 voucher and cash, wanted to keep a £50 dress and return the rest but they are insisting on refunding the gift card before any cash, so I now have to return the entire order, lose more money from delivery and returns costs, and reorder the exact same dress again, just to use the gift card. Absolutely ridiculous, and just one of many problems I've had with them over the years

Title: Good luck getting past the bots
Text: Good luck getting past the bots. When you do, you’re talking to an agent who is also in 10 other conversations, so replies are 10+ minutes. They don’t understand English (sorry but it’s true) and all replies are copy paste. Worst customer service, I’ve never had a good experience and it completely puts me off shopping at asos. They sent me the incorrect item and I’ve spent 30 minutes chasing it. They offered a replacement or refund. I requested replacement, and then was refunded less than I paid for it. What?!

Title: Appalling returns management.
Text: Appalling returns management. This is the second time I've ordered products that I have needed to return and the process has been complicated both times. They make you pay to return product and then don't issue the refund. InPost confirmed return was made on 15th June and I'm still waiting for my money back. It's really poor customer service and I will not be ordering again!!

Title: Not able to self-clear Packages
Text: I've been an ASOS customer for over 15 years. With my recent order, I was asked to pay import duties and taxes of about 35% of the package value through your new carrier, Asendia. Previously, with UPS, I paid the duties and taxes directly to the CBSA, which came to about 15% of the package value. I tried to contact Asendia, but their website doesn't provide any contact option. It would be nice to still have the option to self-clear our packages.

Title: Worst Service in the UK
Text: Honestly where to begin. In simple terms stay away and buy elsewhere. I am sure ASOS are great if all is well but if something goes wrong they’re maybe the worst company in the UK. I bought a pair of timberland boat shoes and had them 7 days when the laces snapped and the leather separated. I wore them twice both in sunshine There so bad when I emailed timberland (The manufacturer) they said sorry and there faulty. ASOS instead said “due to the time passed since you've had the items would lead to this being down to wear and tear rather than a manufacturing fault rain can pull out the natural oils as it dries, shifts the dyes, and can cause stiffening or warping” What a load of nonsense. I emailed back the evidence timberland emailed me and asked that asos contact them. Guess what ASOS have ignored me and three emails later still nothing from them. They don’t care and are money grabbing thieves stay away

Title: Their Customer Service is a nonsense
Text: It took more than 10 days of daily texts with the CS to solve my issue: I needed return labels that I could not get by email. They don’t give any support ticket, so you have to wait, wait, wait for a reply from a human after tearing your hair out because you keep meeting KAI, K for Knucklehead! AI assistant. I gave a 2 because at last and by chance I came across Cory who understood my request and sent in no time those long-awaited labels. Thank you Cory!

Title: Awful company
Text: I ordered the exact same pair of joggers in the same size that I’d bought around three months earlier. The original pair fit perfectly, but the new pair was significantly different in size despite being labelled identically. Because of the inconsistent sizing, I had to pay to return the item after already paying for delivery. I contacted customer service explaining that this wasn’t a case of ordering the wrong size, but a product consistency issue. Rather than acknowledging this or offering any goodwill gesture such as a discount code or free shipping on a replacement, I only received generic responses refusing any compensation. Very disappointing experience for a returning customer.

Title: Loyal customer for years, then my account was closed
Text: I have been an ASOS customer for many years and estimate I’ve spent well over £30,000 with them over that time. The overwhelming majority of my orders arrived without any issues. On only a handful of occasions (around 3–4 times), I reported missing items because they were genuinely missing from my parcels. My final order was over £800, and three items worth around £100 were not included. I reported the missing items in good faith, and the very next day I received an email saying my refund had been refused and my account had been permanently closed. I was accused of breaching their fair use policy with no meaningful explanation or evidence provided. After years of loyalty and tens of thousands of pounds spent, I found it shocking that reporting genuinely missing items resulted in me losing both my account and the refund I believe I was entitled to. To make matters worse, I had already created returns for several recent orders before my account was closed. Thankfully, customer service confirmed those returns would still be honoured, but the whole experience has been incredibly stressful and disappointing. Loyal customers deserve better treatment than this. Based on my experience, I would think twice before placing expensive orders with ASOS

Title: Always a good service
Text: Always a good service

Title: Would give 0 stars if I could
Text: Would give 0 stars if I could! Absolutely disgusting customer service from Boohoo. I placed an order and, after 9 days, there has been absolutely no update whatsoever on the tracking. I contacted Evri directly, who confirmed that my parcel is most likely lost somewhere in their network. I provided Boohoo with screenshots and evidence of this, yet they’ve made no effort to replace my order or issue a refund. Instead, their customer service has been appalling. Responses take days, and when they finally do reply, it’s nothing but the same copy-and-paste response telling me to “wait” – despite the fact that the tracking hasn’t moved in almost 10 days and Evri themselves have admitted they can’t locate the parcel. I’ve now had no choice but to open a dispute with my bank because Boohoo have completely failed to resolve the issue. It’s unacceptable that customers are expected to chase for answers while being ignored and left out of pocket. Avoid this company if you value your time, money, and sanity. Once they’ve got your money, don’t expect any meaningful customer service if something goes wrong.

Title: My query was dealt with efficiently and…
Text: My query was dealt with efficiently and positively with no hassle.

Title: Didn't get order code but money was taken anyway
Text: I didn't receive my order code and my money was taken out of my account but a customer service worker has helped with trying to resolve my issue so thank you kai

Title: Two hours on Customer Care chat!
Text: Never received my allocated birthday discount code before it expired through no fault of my own. Two hours on Customer Care chat, unable to speak directly to a real person, and had to rejoin new chats multiple times. The issue was eventually resolved with a replacement code, but the process was unnecessarily exhausting for what should have been a simple fix.

Title: Parcel made it to Brisbane depot and that's it
Text: Parcel made it to Brisbane depot, then returned to sender. Waited for a long time talking to a chat bot, who was contradicting itself. Was given an answer that the parcel couldn't be delivered to my address or was damaged. Now am $135 out of pocket since June 8 and have to wait for ASOS to receive the parcel back (hasn't left Brisbane yet, according to tracking) and then another 10-20 days before my refund is processed.

Title: Custom service
Text: Its my second time ordering from boohoo and I really like the site. I had a problem with the shipment but Kai really helped me going through it with kindness.

Title: Bad service
Text: I have ordered multiple times from boohoo, and in the beginning 2023/2024 it was always good. Now since a few years the delivery, communication and quality seems to be downgrading. I returned clothes bc it was to small for me and it took almost over 2 months to get me my money back.

Title: i have been using Asos for as long as i…
Text: i have been using Asos for as long as i remember the last 12 months or more i have returned a lot as they have not suited or my hormones were saying no, I am being penalised £9.00 DELIVERY PLUS ANOTHER £4.00 TO SEND BACK ITS ABSOLUTLEY DISCUSSTING. I HAVE JUST ORDERED COST ME £9.00 IT DOSENT FIT IM FRIGTENED TO SEND BACK, SHAME ON YOU ASOS

Title: I ordered clothes and had issued with…
Text: I ordered clothes and had issued with them. I contacted customer service for help with refunding the items and Peryton was extremely helpful, talked me through what I needed to do, and then sorted everything out for me.
Went to your store at Workington Gemma the assistant was extremely helpful and very pleasant to talk with

Title: want to warn others about my experience…
Text: want to warn others about my experience with Missguided. I returned my item using recorded delivery and sent numerous emails regarding my refund. Eventually, Misguided emailed me asking for my order number and my mobile number so they could contact me and sort the matter out. Today, I received a phone call from someone claiming to be calling about my refund. I was told there was no problem and that I would receive my money back. When I asked for the refund to be returned to my PayPal account, I was instead told to go to Google, download an app, and then enter my bank details. I refused because this immediately sounded like a scam then they hung up on me. A genuine refund should be returned to the original payment method, and I would never be comfortable downloading an app or providing my bank details over the phone. This was my personal experience. Please be cautious if you receive a similar call. Always verify who you are speaking to before sharing any personal or financial information PLEASE BEAWARE WHEN BUYING FROM  MISSGUIDED

Title: Late with deliveries
Text: Late with deliveries, slow processing orders, and quite unapologetic about that. My item went out of stock while waiting to be shipped!!! Took over 5 days to just process the order. Just awful!

Title: Very damaged box through transit
Text: I ordered a pair of shoes took over a week to be delivered, when shoes arrived it looked like the box with shoes in has been thrown around with a hole in the top and like it's been sat on not happy with the customer service absolutely ridiculous.

Title: Spencer was absolutely brilliant thanks…
Text: Spencer was absolutely brilliant thanks so much for your assistance and support with solving my inquiry

Title: Bought a dress for a party
Text: Bought a dress for a party. Did not come in time and didn’t even arrive. I payed for insurance and the insurance didn’t even want to accept the request for a refund. Title: Incompetent and lying
Text: I returned a faulty pair of New Balance shoes using an official ASOS return label (Order No: 928896677). The return was successfully tracked and delivered back to them on April 26, 2026, via Hermes DE / ReBOUND Tracking No: 70116145000177. ASOS has officially confirmed they received the return, yet they are withholding my money. I have contacted customer support multiple times over several months and have been given completely conflicting information. Agents have claimed my refund was issued in April, then May 20th, and then June 2nd. No money has ever reached my account. ASOS refuses to provide an Acquirer Reference Number (ARN) or any transaction proof to back up these dates. They have my returned shoes and my money, and front-line chat support does nothing but read from scripted lies. Absolutely unacceptable service. I expect an escalation manager to contact me immediately with a valid banking reference number or my actual refund.

Title: How a Damaged Parcel Turned Into a…
Text: How a Damaged Parcel Turned Into a Permanently Closed Account I hadn’t ordered from ASOS for several years before placing this order. When my parcel arrived, it had a hole in the packaging and two makeup items were missing. I contacted ASOS immediately, sent photographs of the damaged parcel, and followed the instructions I was given. ASOS confirmed that my order should have contained four items. After I confirmed that only two had arrived, they approved a refund for the missing products and told me it would be paid within 10 working days. I thought the matter had been resolved. A few days later, I received an email saying that my refund had been refused, my account had been permanently closed, and that my account activity was not in line with ASOS’s Fair Use Policy. This came as a complete surprise. I had simply reported a damaged parcel and followed ASOS’s own process. When I tried to understand what had happened, I discovered that there was no real opportunity to discuss the decision with anyone. Instead, I was informed that any further emails about the matter would be automatically closed without a response, leaving me with no way of asking questions or explaining my case. Wanting to understand the reason for such a serious decision, I submitted a Data Subject Access Request. The documents ASOS provided confirmed exactly what I had told them from the beginning: that I had reported the damaged parcel, provided photographs, and that their own system had approved the refund. Despite this, I was never given any explanation as to why the refund was reversed or what evidence led ASOS to conclude that my account activity was not in line with their Fair Use Policy. I was simply told that it was a business decision. The money was never the real issue. What disappointed me was the complete lack of transparency. I followed ASOS’s instructions, relied on the refund approval communicated by their own system, and was ultimately left without either the approved refund or any meaningful explanation as to why that decision had been reversed or why my account had been permanently closed.

Text: I paid for next-day delivery. Instead, I got two hours of customer service and no answers. Title: Mammoth return charges!!
Text: Boohoo have somehow managed to turn the simple act of returning an item into a masterclass in daylight robbery. You order clothes, they don’t fit, you send them back — fair enough. But then the real performance begins. Delivery charges, return charges, and then mysterious “additional charges” slapped on top like some backstreet accountant making it up as they go along. Over £20 gone. Vanished. And for what exactly? Nobody at Boohoo seems capable of explaining where these extra costs even come from. You ask for clarification and get the corporate equivalent of shrugging shoulders and hiding behind templated emails. It’s like dealing with a bookmaker after a dodgy VAR decision — all confidence when taking your money, complete silence when asked to justify it. The whole thing feels deliberately opaque. They lure people in with cheap prices, then claw the money back through hidden charges and disgraceful customer service. If this is their idea of “customer care,” they may as well just put a sign on the website saying: “Thanks for your money — now good luck getting any of it back.” Absolute shambles of a company. Avoid if you value transparency, fairness, or basic respect as a customer.

Text: Worst customer service I have ever dealt with, a 1 star review is too kind. I placed an order for a matching hoodie and shorts coord. I was sent a dress instead of the hoodie. I took photos and contacted customer service, as their website instructs. They sent me a returns link. I returned the whole order. There is no option for an exchange. I was charged £1.99 to make the return as I "chose to return the shorts" even though I only bought them because they matched the hoodie that I never got. They then issued a refund for £22 (minus the returns charge) where I received 2/3 of it direct to my bank and 1/3 to a gift card - no idea why. I never got the gift card. Had to contact them again for this. I then realised they never refunded the original postage costs, which I had to argue with them for for over a week, quoting consumer rights law, until they finally refunded that. There is no continuity with who is dealing with your issue with a new person responding every time, so overall it has taken over a month of arguing with them to return my money because they sent me the wrong item. And I'm still at a two quid loss. I am going to purchase the cheapest item they sell to use the gift card I never wanted and I will never be shopping at Boohoo again.

Title: Awful. Shein would be better
Text: Awful, awful, awful. I've been a loyal ASOS customer for the past 2–3 years, but the decline in quality has been incredibly disappointing. The prices they're charging compared to the quality you actually receive should honestly be considered unlawful. At this point, you might as well shop at Shein; in fact, that's exactly what I'll be doing if this is the standard of quality I can expect. The biggest issue is that the poor quality often isn't immediately obvious. It typically becomes apparent only after a couple of weeks of wearing the item, by which point you're already approaching the end of the return window. ASOS only allows returns within 28 days of the delivery date, which gives customers even less time to identify these issues. The industry standard is typically a return window of 14–30 days to return an item once the return window opens. Save your money and shop elsewhere. Quality is awful for what you pay for. The return policy feels misleading in practice, and customer service is virtually non-existent.

Title: Very disappointed with my experience
Text: Very disappointed with my experience. My order was given an estimated delivery date of 20 June 2026, yet on the day of delivery I was informed that the order was still being processed and had not even been dispatched. It is frustrating to provide customers with a delivery estimate when the item has not left the warehouse. Communication has been poor, and I have been left without a clear explanation for the delay. If there are stock or processing issues, customers should be informed promptly rather than having to chase for updates. I understand that delays can happen, but transparency and accurate delivery information are essential. Unfortunately, this experience has left me with little confidence in the service. This has been one of the most frustrating online shopping experiences I have had. I placed this order because I needed it for an important event the following day. I relied on the estimated delivery date provided by the company and planned accordingly. Imagine my frustration when, on the supposed delivery date, I was informed that my order had not even been dispatched and was still being processed. It is completely unacceptable to advertise delivery estimates that the company has no realistic prospect of meeting. If an order has not left the warehouse, customers should not be led to believe it will arrive on a specific date. What made matters worse was the response from customer service. After the inconvenience, disappointment and stress caused by this delay, the manager appeared to think that refunding the express shipping fee would somehow resolve the issue. The issue was never the delivery charge. The issue was that the item did not arrive when promised and was therefore useless for the event for which it was purchased. Communication throughout has been poor. I received no proactive update regarding the delay and was left chasing for information myself. Had I been informed earlier, I could have made alternative arrangements. Instead, I was left scrambling at the last minute because the company failed to provide accurate information about my order. Delays happen, but misleading delivery expectations, poor communication, and a lack of accountability are much harder to excuse. This experience has completely undermined my confidence in the company, and I will be very reluctant to place another order in the future.

I than email and was told to contact by email today - Saturday - if no parcel delivered by friday. I was told now that I have to wait till EOD day today as they want to give fair amount of time to courier for fair assessment. Seems 7 days since they discovered that there was a fault is not fair time... It is therefore more important to give fair treatment to a courier that lost and damanged my parcel than to a customer who paid for it.... I will of course escalate it further but this is the worst customer service I have experienced ever in UK... I still had in my mind Asos from long tiem ago - please be aware this is not same company and customer is not important. Title: Warranty claim denied despite legal obligation – avoid customer service
Text: I purchased Adidas Originals Firebird Shorts from ASOS in July 2025. After less than 11 months, the seams started coming apart. I reported this on 3 June 2026. What followed was one of the worst customer service experiences I have had with any retailer: •	The chat assistant confirmed a replacement would be arranged and that a human agent would follow up within 24 hours. I waited 5 days. •	I was then contacted by four different agents across multiple emails, none of whom had read the prior conversation. •	I was asked to resubmit photos I had already sent via chat. •	Every single response rejected my claim as “wear and tear” – with zero legal or factual basis provided. Under German consumer law (§ 477 BGB), any defect appearing within the first 12 months of purchase is legally presumed to be a manufacturing fault. The burden of proof lies with the seller, not the customer. ASOS never addressed this argument once across multiple emails. I escalated formally and set a 7-day deadline. The response was another copy-paste rejection from yet another agent who clearly had not read my email. I have now filed a complaint with the Verbraucherzentrale Berlin and the EU ODR platform. I will not be using ASOS again.

⭐☆☆☆☆ I have been an ASOS customer for years, but my recent experiences have been incredibly disappointing. My latest issue involved returning a parcel through an InPost locker. I followed the return process correctly, placed the parcel into the locker, and the compartment door locked shut. The locker screen then froze before any proof of postage could be generated. I immediately reported the issue, took a photograph of the frozen screen, contacted InPost multiple times, revisited the locker when advised to do so, and escalated the matter to InPost’s Escalations Team. InPost confirmed in writing that because this was an ASOS return, ASOS needed to contact them directly to raise an investigation. Despite providing this information to ASOS, I spent hours being passed between agents, repeating myself, and being asked to provide proof of postage that was never generated due to the technical fault. Why can’t asos just raise this with inpost? You have the parcel tracking number and the order number. I was eventually escalated to a manager who acknowledged that the issue was caused by a technical fault and not by anything I had done. However, ASOS still refused to raise an investigation with InPost and instead directed me back to them. The most frustrating part has been the lack of ownership and accountability. As a customer, I have been left stuck between ASOS and InPost, with each company directing me to the other. I understand that technical faults happen, but customers should not be left to resolve disputes between two companies when they have followed the correct process from the start.

Title: They use their fair use policy as a…
Text: They use their fair use policy as a scapegoat for bad customer service. When a parcel arrived with a missing item, I contacted asos to let them know. I then received an email to say my account had been closed and I could no longer order from them. Erm for their mistake?! My previous purchase was 10 months before and I kept it. So to say they find my behaviour out of line with their fair use policy is honestly unbelievable. Can we remind them they operate an online business and ether it’s a surprise to them that different brands size up differently so it’s very common (and in my case) for people to order the same item in different sizes. And for me to have a completely clean record, an account with them for 5+ years (I’ll admit I’m an infrequent buyer). And for me to raise one complaint and suddenly I’m out of pocket. No no, ASOS do better.  Also the fact there is no way to contact a person. Only an AI chatbot. I’m sure because they know what they are doing on such a grand scale is morally corrupt.

Title: Wrong item received
Text: Ordered an item. They sent wrong item. Asked to return, they said they’ll think about refund based on inspection. Trust lost.

Title: Ordered trousers and received a crop top
Text: I ordered two trousers off boohoo costing €35 including delivery. My package arrived and I received one trousers which was nothing like the pictures and instead of the other trousers I got sent a bikini crop top. Got onto customer service and got 12 different customer service reps replying with the same generic message and asking for more proof and pictures of the trousers. Couldn’t send a picture of the trousers because I didn’t get them. Explained this in each email and sent pictures of the top and each time I get a generic reply and then new customer service reps asking for the same again. I finally admitted defeat and gave up and just returned everything. They then issued a refund and failed to refund me the delivery because they said that’s not refundable…even though I paid for delivery for items I didn’t receive. An additional charge was taken for return too. Honestly do not buy from the website. I’ve had better customer experiences on SHEIN….absolutely painful experience. Forgot to mention it took a solid 7 weeks of forward and back emails before I gave up.

Title: An absolute awful company
Text: An absolute awful company! I ordered some shoes and within seconds of ordering I realised it was getting delivered to my old address. I quickly contacted them and asked them to either change or cancel my order as I wouldn’t be able to go to my old address as it’s hours away. I was told they couldn’t change or cancel my order so now my order is going to end up completely lost! Most companies will change or simply cancel your order but not this company!!!! Do not use! Customer service if you can call it that also take ages to respond!

Title: Amazing
Text: We love shopping from Asos

Title: Awful experience!
Text: Awful experience!! I won’t be ordering from Boohoo again. I’m really disappointed with the whole experience. A £15 “protection” charge was added to my basket, which I did not want and did not knowingly agree to. This should not be automatically added, and it certainly should not be down to the customer to pay extra to cover Boohoo’s own packing mistakes or issues if a delivery goes missing. I only noticed the charge once the order had already been processed, which feels very misleading and unfair. The customer service has been just as frustrating. It’s so annoying dealing with automated AI responses rather than a real person who can actually understand and resolve the issue properly. On top of that, two items were missed from my return refund, so I’m now having to chase money that should have been refunded correctly in the first place. Overall, this has been a really poor experience from start to finish.. unexpected charges, poor customer service, and refund issues.

Title: I order package 8 June till now I…
Text: I order package 8 June till now I didn’t receive nothing even email or order number They don’t wanna send me money back …. Frustrating and very bad customer service 😡😡😡

Text: This company is so poor. First they delayed my package shipment by two weeks making me miss the event I needed the clothes for and had to make last minute plans. When I tried to cancel the package (keep in mind it hadn't even shipped yet) they told me there was nothing that could be done. After processing the return, now I am dealing with another hassle with this company as they claim that one of the items I bought was not included in the return. The package wasn't even opened! It went from my mailbox right back to the UPS drop off. Avoid this company at all costs.

Title: rejected a return wrongfully
Text: I accidentally ordered an item twice and tried to return one of the duplicates, which had never been tried on. The return was rejected on the grounds that the item had dirt marks - marks I believe came from a previous customer, not me. When I contacted customer service to appeal, they shut the conversation down without giving me a chance to explain. I won't be ordering from them again, which is disappointing after being a loyal customer for over 10 years." Main fixes: split the run-on opening sentence so the "ordered it twice" and "never tried on" details land separately, clarified that the rejection happened because of the dirt marks (the original left it ambiguous), and smoothed the ending so the "10 years" point lands with more weight.

Title: Poor Handling of a Manufacturing Defect
Text: I attempted to make a claim with ASOS for an On product that developed what I believe to be a manufacturing defect. After contacting On directly, I was advised that ASOS, as the retailer, would be responsible for handling the claim. My claim was initially rejected because the item had been worn and was outside the standard returns window. This reasoning makes little sense—how would a manufacturing defect become apparent without the item being worn and used? The situation then became even more frustrating when ASOS returned the item to an address I have not lived at for over seven years. The current occupant understandably refused delivery, and I now have no idea where the item is. I am left without the product and without a refund. Under the Consumer Rights Act, consumers have statutory rights regarding faulty goods that extend beyond a retailer’s standard returns policy. Despite this, ASOS has failed to properly address my claim or provide a satisfactory resolution. I have spent far more time chasing this issue than any customer should reasonably be expected to, and there appears to be little effort from ASOS to resolve the matter. Very disappointing customer service and complaint handling.

Title: The same as everyone else. Returns are an absolute scam
Text: The same as everyone else. They don’t send the returns labels then refuse to let you return it even when they’re in fault. Absolute joke! What the hell as happened to ASOS?

Title: I returned a couple of tops which were…
Text: I returned a couple of tops which were badly made and I'll fitting. I did not select to be refunded to a gift card. However this is exactly what I was given. A gift card to be used within 3 months. I got in touch with boohoo to explain I did not choose to be refunded to a gift card and could I be refunded to my original payment method instead and the answer was no. Therefore I will never order from boohoo again.

Title: First time customer - great experience
Text: As a first time customer of ASOS, I almost didnt buy on their website because of the reviews I read on here. My expectations were very low. However, I have chosen to leave a review because my experience has been very good. I didnt bother ordering with express delivery after reading these reviews, so waited for standard service. It took a week but I did get an email saying sorry for the delay from ASOS. When it was dispatched, I had regular updates. The clothing was much better quality than I was expecting, esp for the price. All 4 items were in pristine condition. Unfortunately I had ordered too large a size. I arranged a return and it was very easy - all items could go in one parcel and I got a QR code for royal mail. Asos helped me find my nearest drop off point and a sent a confirmation email that it was with Royal Mail. I can even track my return. My confidence has been restored in ASOS, so much so that I have ordered again the same items in a smaller size (which I could have exchanged free of charge). I have already had an email saying my items are dispatched and I'm confident they will arrive shortly.

Title: The worst customer service
Text: The worst customer service. I paid for a premier account but then still had to pay a delivery charge. I’ve been told the premier takes 1 hour to activate however this is not stated in the terms and conditions, nor is it communicated at any point of subscription. Unfortunately I’ve been waiting 2 weeks to resolve the matter and try to get a refund. It’s impossible to speak with a human it’s all live chat communication with unhelpful AI

Title: Some good deals but bad delivery
Text: Okay to review regarding this company is that it can offer some good deals which I'm happy with when I can find a good deal. You are totally let down by your delivery company I haven't ordered for over two years and I recently ordered you have changed your delivery company to a company called Relay. I receive the message in advance regarding delivery and I replied with "13th Floor both lifts work" which gives an indication of how I would like to delivery to happen. I live in a block of flats 132 flats the driver arrived and I said please come to the 13th Floor when I opened the door via the intercom for the driver the driver deliberately left my goods downstairs in the communal area where any Tom, Richard and Harry can pick up my items and walk off with it. Based on this interaction I do not believe I will be buying anything from ASOS again. You've change your delivery company to an even worse delivery company which will make you lose customers.

Title: I received a 25% discount voucher code…
Text: I received a 25% discount voucher code on the 14th June SUNDAZE, I found the email Saturday the 15th June on my way home from travelling and thought to myself I will use it tomorrow. This morning I went to use it and it wouldn't work so I had a communication with ASOS chatbot who didn't understand why so he put me through to a human customer service representative. After about 40 minutes being asked to wait she came back and told me the code was only valid for 12 hours. It was all a waist of my time. It did say in the small print limited time but not 12 hours only. Really bad service all round.

Title: No customer service available
Text: No customer service available, only a FAQ and a chatbot. Spent half a day to get in contact with an actual person, who couldn't help me anyway. Due to different circumstances the return window had expired by a few days but they showed no interest in finding a solution or an acceptable compromise. I know they followed their policy but many comparable companies have some interest in keeping their customers.

Title: FedEx sucks
Text: I’ve always loved ASOS, but ever since they changed their delivery to FedEx - my packages have been delivered super late.

Title: Absolutely terrible service from ASOS
Text: Absolutely terrible service from ASOS. Purchased a virtual gift card for my mum for her birthday, gift card never received. Proceeded to message them now 6 times and going around in circles with AI and the service agents just say the vouchers team will get back to you. 8 days later, still no voucher. Do not purchase the virtual vouchers because you’ll never get it and they’ll steal your money! Very disappointed.

Title: I had to give one star as would not…
Text: I had to give one star as would not leave me 0. Please do not order from here, clothes not that good, but return policy is shocking, they claim you can return by Evri but you cant and cost of return is £10, this is a joke.

Title: I never received my money back
Text: Over the past 4 years, I have contacted ASOS customer support multiple times regarding unresolved refunds for two orders. Each time I was told to wait and that someone would contact me once a solution was found. However, I was never contacted by anyone, and I had to repeatedly reach out myself without any real resolution. During this time, I also offered multiple alternative solutions, including refunding to a different international bank account, PayPal, or issuing the refund as store credit/voucher. None of these options were ever implemented, and I never received my money back. Despite this situation, I continued to shop with ASOS and remained a loyal customer for a long time. Unfortunately, after years of unsuccessful communication and no resolution, I was effectively refused my refund. In my view, this situation does not comply with my rights as a consumer, as the company failed to provide a proper refund or a viable alternative solution within a reasonable timeframe. I am disappointed with how this case was handled and the lack of follow-through on repeated promises to resolve the issue, and I can no longer continue as a customer of this company.

Title: ASOS have become a joke at this point
Text: ASOS have a fantastic range of fashion items, but what use is that if they partner with a courier like Evri that fails to deliver. Twice this month Evri have lost my ASOS items, including a dress needed to attend a wedding abroad. As a longtime ASOS Premier customer I am now reluctant to place order because I am doubtful it will be received. And then all ASOS offer for support is a useless chatbot system that confirms how little customers are valued. ASOS is at risk of becoming a joke and faded memory if they continue to refuse to be customer focused. Take the hint from a management consultant and transformation specialist.

Title: Impossible deleting a “social” account. Time wasted. Frustrated and annoyed.
Text: For some reason I ended up with a social account that was connected to my Apple ID. As a result, my ASOS emails kept going to my work email. This made organising my affairs very messy. After much time wasting via the so-called helpline, I discovered that resetting my password was impossible and I kept getting the “access denied” message. I was going round in circles which was very infuriating. Finally, I had no option but to delete my account and lose all of the benefits that I had built up. The help Centre is no help. They just fob you off and ask you to come back 24 hours later. I feel very frustrated and disappointed that I have lost all my “perks”.

Title: No morals
Text: Be very careful when you buy from Boohoo and you return an item. It automatically defaults to receive a “gift card” as a refund. If that’s not bad enough if you do not spend the “gift card” within 3 months - the “gift card” gets cancelled and you lose the money. Why they call it a gift card is something I do not understand. It’s not a gift card - it’s my money! Disgusting service and a complete con. I have asked them why it does not default to the payment method you have paid with and they cannot answer. Well I can - it’s a way of you either spending money with them again or if you cannot find anything within the 3 months to buy - they keep the money! You should be ashamed Boohoo.

Title: 'Ethical' fast fashion?
Text: Rapanui make attractive looking clothes, with a casual, surfy, nature-themed look. Unfortunately they refuse to publish slightly critical product reviews on their website, creating a false appearance of entirely happy customers. For instance my fair, truthful, 3* review of a jacket with a badly designed hood has not been published, and I'm unable to re-submit any review for this product as they tell me I've already submitted my review. It's apparently an ethical brand, and around 10 years ago when I was first started wearing their clothes, there was a big emphasis on their relationship with an Indian textile factory committed to fair labour practices.  However, more products are listed as being made in China (not known for particularly good labour laws & human rights) with no further explanation of their labour sourcing, leading me to assume their focus is more on the materials (i.e. organic cotton) and more environmentally friendly processes. Where Rapanui let down is on durability.  I have over time had to throw a lot of boxers away, & send a large number of t-shirts to be re-milled. Their circular fashion concept is their way of keeping the prices fairly low, & it looks appealing on paper that you'll get a coupon for returning, but it's only cost effective if you're already buying over £50 worth of goods, otherwise the £5 off voucher (you can only use one at a time)  is absorbed by the nearly-equivalent delivery fee. And often, I've had trouble with the codes, and it has taken a while for customer service to get back to me, but to be fair they have always resolved it. On balance I still like some of what they do, and buy the odd item, but less wedded to the brand these days. Because most of their t shirts are a lighter weight 155 g/m2 I've been looking for higher thread-count items but these are hard to come by. Even though I question how sustainable they are, there's not many or any competitors on the same level with a similar offering, so I intend to keep Rapanui on my radar for clothes shopping, for the meanwhile.

Title: Amazing service from Sophie!
Text: My Barents jumper was just perfect in every way, but this review is about Finisterre customer service, which is just impossibly excellent. After having the jumper for a few months, I had a small issue with aftercare which could have been my fault. Finisterre were just brilliant, answering emails almost instantaneously, and offering to replace the jumper with no quibbles, and even paying for the return. This is way more than I expected!  Particular kudos goes to the inestimable Sophie; she's a real credit to you Finisterre and genuinely deserves recognition (and a bonus!). Thanks

Customer service is now refusing to help because the return was over a month ago. Essentially, they have kept my items and my money. Double-check your return receipts! EDIT: I sent a complaint email to customerservices@ boohoo.com and they refunded me for the two items they initially failed to process. Would encourage you all to do the same if you've faced a similar issue

Title: The next day delivery is not…
