Title: “Don’t Charge Us to Advertise for You!”
Text: I am outraged by Primark’s decision to start charging 15p for paper bags. This isn’t about sustainability; it’s an obvious money grab at the expense of customers. These bags are flimsy, tear easily, and have your branding plastered all over them—essentially, we’re being asked to pay for the privilege of being walking billboards for your company. This charge feels completely unjustified. Customers should never be forced to pay for something so low-quality and disposable. If you truly cared about sustainability, you would offer durable, reusable bags, not flimsy ones that break after a single use. Charging customers for these bags preys on our trust, and it’s clear that this is just another way to increase profits under the guise of “helping the environment.” What’s even worse is that this unfair practice is now becoming common across retailers. But I refuse to accept this as the new normal. As customers, we need to stand up and reject any company that tries to profit off us by charging for bags that have no real value. Primark should be ashamed of this decision, and I will no longer shop at a store that disrespects its customers this way. I strongly encourage everyone to stop supporting this exploitative practice. Don’t let Primark—or any other retailer—think they can get away with charging us for something that should be part of our shopping experience without an extra cost. Refuse to accept it. Speak up. Demand better.

Title: Shame on you Primark for charging 15p…
Text: Shame on you Primark for charging 15p on paper bags, plastic is supposed to be ruining the environment & had the plastic levy, pure greed, gone are the traditional Primark days your NOT value on £ anymore your actually expensive now, you don’t think ppl are noticing but we actually are!

Title: Disappointing Checkout Experience at Primark Llanelli
Text: Visited the Llanelli Primark store recently to pick up some clothing. The shopping experience started off well—the range of clothes was reasonable, and the changing rooms were spacious and easy to access. However, things took a sharp downturn at the checkout. The entire checkout process was poorly designed and frustrating. The area appears to have been recently revamped, but not for the better. There was only one staff member handling sales and returns at a very small counter, and another stationed at a separate area where customers were funneled into a queue to use self-service checkouts. The self-checkouts themselves were confusing and inconsistent—some had handheld scanners, others did not. Trying to scan clothes, pay extra for a bag, and locate a printed receipt turned into a chaotic task. To make matters worse, as we attempted to exit, we were abruptly stopped and spoken to rudely by a staff member. Apparently, you’re required to scan your receipt in order to leave the area—something that was never made clear during the process. It left a sour taste after what had been a decent in-store experience. A little more signage, clearer instructions, and better staffing could make a world of difference. Until then, be prepared for a stressful finish to your shopping trip.

She finally did remove them folded them up and placed them in the bag. I paid for the items and started to leave the till point, when Security came running upto the till point. Title: Bracknell customer service experience.
Text: Went to Bracknell store and bought a set of underwear and socks. The customer assistant behind the counter was as rude and I’ll mannered as I’ve ever seen. Said come here sir and proceeded to scan and literally throw my goods into a paper bag and ask me for the money which I paid by card for then threw my receipt in the bag. Then I realised that she’d over charged me and asked her why she charged me £11.00 for goods that were marked £5.00 on the pack. She looked at me like I had just insulted her and called her manager over for help. He explained what to do if it happened again and she stood behind the counter and argued with him about it in front of me and other customers and staff. This lady has completely the wrong attitude to be working in retail and will not ever be an asset to primark or any retailer. She has absolutely no customer service acumen or service skills. Just because Primark goods are cheaper than other retailers doesn’t mean that Primark customers should be treated like 2nd class citizens and be spoken to and treated in such a rude, dismissive and disrespectful manner. Absolutely disgusting behaviour!

Title: Primark West Bromwich
Text: Primark West Bromwich, never had any issues but this time the customer services and managers were horrible, think they were in a rush. I bought a pair of Disney Mickey mouse black school shoes from the school section of the shop for 4yr old. My DD went to school and came back with the bleeding at the back of feet just after 1 day of wear. I bought her another pair from a different shop. When I went back to return the shoe to Primark, the lady at the till made faces and gossiped to her colleague that if she or her kids have this issue she won't return them but buy them other pair from CLARKS. Then came the lady manager and her statement was: those shoes are not the school shoe but the fashion wear and parents should buy from big brands to have comfort shoes. So I felt they were promoting other brands and if they knew these shoes are the fashion shoe they should have put it on clearly and not on the school section, so that customers shouldn't buy them. None of the staff showed any kindness or concern,no responsibility of misleading information. None of them offered any exchange or any gift card. Rubbish customer service and manager statement was legendary. So PLEASE DON'T BUY DISNEY MICKEY MOUSE SHOE FROM PRIMARK FOR SCHOOL.

Title: Management should be ashamed of themselves!
Text: I will be honest, but I will find it very difficult to be constructive, as I have just discovered that Primark's customer service is shocking.  They do not even understand the meaning of the term.  I am a 67 year old lady who lives alone and have no family.  Unfortunately I was rushed to hospital 3 weeks ago and taken into resus with heart problems, before being admitted for 2 weeks, This meant that I was unable to return some goods to Primark within the 28 day timeframe.  I have spent several hours calling not only Head Office but also 4 stores to ask if I could have a couple more days to return the goods, as I am not long out of hospital.  Nobody ever answers the phone, even at Head Office.  In fact you are cut off after it rings a few times.  Unlike Next and M & S and Roman, Primark does not "have a heart" and will not allow me to return £20 worth of goods a little late - even though I can provide proof.  How could I possibly return on time, when I have been "blue lighted" with sirens into hospital?  I have been told that you are unable to help.  Your staff could not even wish me all the best.  What sort of training do you give your staff?  I am mortified to learn that this is what you think of your customers.  I am seriously thinking of taking this to the papers.

Title: eyelash glue

Title: Poor Service, Awful In-store Experience - Why bother when there is better
Text: I was told if I was to contact customer services that you would be able to help. This week I tried to return some items that I bought before my holidays and I was refused the return or exchange for items I had taken to the till due to them being slightly outside the 28 return date. I explained to the store attendant and then supervisor that I am 28 weeks pregnant and have been poorly and unable to return these items. The items I wanted to return total between £50- 60 which is a lot to lose with items that don't fit me and a few that didn't fit my children. As you are aware there is a bit of a culture with Primark that encourages us to spend freely with lower prices, however I am so disappointed that I have done that and been met with such a strict returns policy. As a bit of insight I would like to say I was 25 weeks pregnant when I bought these items, your changing rooms on the women's wear level were closed, the store was extremely hot, staff directed me to other changing rooms that were just as busy and then then I queued for about 30 minutes to make my purchase. Its really hard and a challenge to shop there when you are not pregnant never mind pregnant. At the very least I was only expecting to exchange - no more but I travelled all the way to Warrington to have a wasted journey and it just really upset me as other retailers (like New Look) didn't even ask they just did the returns without a query. I was even more disappointed that the human factor didn't exist to understand the challenges as a pregnant woman in your store has had and why I just bought items without trying on. With other retailers relaxing their returns policy, some going to unlimited! It’s clear that it encourages repeat purchases that are far more valuable. PRIMARK VALUE NO ONE just want you in and out of their stores and your money. Zero service. Get to a better shop, plenty that have better quality online and in stores like new look that deliver to your door and don’t have this issue. First store that I’ve ever known not to even exchange with a receipt even after the date! It was a day or two!

Title: Primark Centre:MK POOR SERVICE
Text: We recently visited the centre:MK primark and was very disappointed and disgusted at the attitude and laziness of the staff. Both myself and my husband visited the beauty section to have our eyebrows waxed. There was a lady in front of us asking for the same service… the member of staff on the reception couldn’t be less enthusiastic. She point blank said no to the lady I. Front of us.. and to myself and my husband… saying they were busy when the whole of the beauty section was completely empty. I am bringing this behaviour to your attention because your staff are turning away paying customers just to have an easy day. But are giving your company a very bad reputation for poor customer service

Title: Great customer service
Text: I took my 9 year old niece to Penneys to shop with her birthday money and she collected a basket at the door and filled it and we went to the checkout to a very nice sales assistant who checked in my niece's purchases.  When we got home I found the receipt contained an item that we had not picked up, I figured it had been left at the bottom of the shopping basket/bag and we did not see it before we went shopping, it was something my niece had no use for, and was too young to use it.  I sent a photo of the item along with a copy of the receipt to Customer Services Primark and they promptly replied and subsequently sent a e-gift card for twice the price of the item.  Thank you Primark.

Title: Buyers beware
Text: Went to return a couple of items. The first were socks that had rubber around the rim of the sock to help them stay up and the second was a shirt. The socks had caused a dermatitis where the rubber had contact with my skin and the shirt had a pen mark on it that I hadn’t noticed on purchase. The customer services assistant Karam called her manager. The manager, Samya, told me that the socks couldn’t have caused me dermatitis as no other customers had complained. With regards to the pen mark, she told me that items are meticulously checked on the shop floor and an item would never be sold with a pen mark on it. I had to argue with her before she issued the returns but her attitude remained poor and argumentative.

Title: Non-colourfast dyes; they run, they stain!
Text: Decent stores, nice staff (where I've visted). The clothes are unsurprisingly very bad and not for the reason you might first expect. The problem is the dyes. The stitching of clothes is usually great and the materials are fairly good, but none of the garments have good quality fast dyes. End result? After a few washes your garment fades, or worse; if it is black, navy or dark grey it will end up with brownish red stains where the red dye has run and the others haven't. This actually makes Primark terrible value for money as you'd need to replace every garment that does this far more often than if you'd paid double the price elsewhere. Think, one pair of shorts from Primark lasting a month or two versus a well made pair with good dyes lasting one or two years, perhaps longer. I won't shop here again. I don't shop here exclusively, but this is one of the few stores I keep having to buy replacements for. Matalan was the same. Cheap dyes ruin otherwise good clothing. Bad value. Bad quality non-colourfast dyes. There really is no point stepping foot in this shop. Note: all washed per the label unstructions (40°C) & washed with similar colours, inside out.

Title: Outdated Returns process
Text: I wish I could give it less than one star …. I have kids and shop at Primark frequently - yesterday I tried to exchange a pair of shorts (all labels and tags still on) my daughter had bought the week before for a larger size …didn’t have the receipt - but had the payment on my card which I tried to show the cashier ….she agreed she could see it was a straight exchange but would need her supervisor - I could see the supervisor approaching and even before she heard what I needed - she was clear - no receipt - no refund ! I was very kind and calm and replied that that was ok - I didn’t want a refund, I wanted a simple exchange - to which she responded - No - no receipt - no exchange, then walked off. The cashier was so embarrassed - especially as I had a full basket with £140 quids worth of clothes in ! I told her to keep the shorts and donate them - but she had to refuse as it was against policy ! The whole thing was outrageous and old fashioned - most shops work on barcodes now - not paper receipts … Primark needs to adapt and evolve and put some effort into training their staff to be more professional, empathic and kind !!

Title: Appalling customer service
Text: Appalling customer service. There is no telephone help whatsoever at the Harrogate store, or indeed the Head Office. I have tried no less than 9 times to make contact, over the past week, the line rings out for a short period of time, and then the line clears down, BEFORE anyone speaks to the customer. Resorting to e-mail is hardly any better because I have had around 3 responses to my e-mail from the same advisor,   who doesn't seem to be able to understand what I am trying to ask!  Auto- pilot responses are no good! All I wanted was to request an extension of a few days extra( to the date on my receipt) to return an item which didn't fit.I have to drive almost 30 miles to the nearest Primark store, and it would have helped me over an emergency.   I have spent a lot of money at Primark over the years, and am disgusted that they simply couldn't care less about the customer's problems, and are unwilling to put themselves out in order to give any support I won't be shopping there again. .

Title: Terrible refund policy
Text: I shop in Primark a lot and generally the quality of the clothes is good and better priced than other high street retailers, although the sizing of their clothes is all over the place often meaning I have to go up two sizes in jeans - that’s fine I expect it now, but their refund policy is shocking. I bought some jeans that didn’t fit and a bodysuit that was too short even though it was the right size on the label, I’ve searched everywhere for the receipt and can’t find it. Their refund policy is that you have to have the *original till receipt* not *proof of purchase* as you do with other retailers. Sometimes people lose their receipts, life happens! Luckily the value of my items I wanted to return was only £16 but if I were a person in financial hardship like so many people are these days, this would be a lot of money to suck up! I tried to ask if they would send me a receipt or if I called the store with my transaction info would they reprint it - I got repetitive answers basically stating their refund policy which I could find on the back of my till receipt…which is lost. I’m going to have to start trying clothes in the changing rooms each time from now on as I don’t want to risk losing my receipt god forbid.

Title: My review is about return policy
Text: My review is about return policy. There are few, if any, stores in NY that only have a 28 day return policy. At the very least the return window is 30 days. Being used to that policy, I went to Primark to return something at the 30 day mark....two days late. The cashier told me she could not help me; that the policy is a strict 28 days. I could not get my money back for the items that fit no one, nor could I even exchange them for some other merchandise. After I returned home, I called the store manager and spoke to the one on duty at that time. She said they do make exceptions for a day or two, so she told me I could come back the same day and she would help me. I am a senior citizen, had just recently gotten home from the store and really did not feel able to go out again. I told her I could come back in the morning, but she said she would only allow the refund til end of day. So even though I am tired out, I have to get myself back to the store; spend more money to get there and more time. Their return policy is narrower than any other and I will not be shopping for anyone in that store again.
